name: HTTP Enumeration Scripts
description: Runs HTTP-specific NSE scripts to enumerate directories, check headers, and discover allowed methods.
expected_output:
| http-enum:
|   /admin/: Admin panel
|   /robots.txt: Robots file
| http-methods:
|   Supported Methods: GET HEAD POST OPTIONS PUT DELETE
use_case: Quick web enumeration without specialized tools — useful when only nmap is available.

name: NetBIOS Name Scan
description: Scans a network range for NetBIOS names, revealing Windows hostnames and workgroup/domain membership.

arguments.-r: Use local port 137 for scans (required for some networks)

expected_output:
192.168.1.10    DC01            CORP    <server>
192.168.1.20    WORKSTATION1    CORP    <unique>
192.168.1.30    FILESERVER      CORP    <unique>
use_case: Quickly map Windows hosts on a network segment — reveals hostnames and domain membership.
detections: Multiple NBT-NS queries from single source

name: Web Technology Fingerprinting
description: Identifies web technologies, CMS, frameworks, server software, and plugins used by a website.
expected_output: http://target [200 OK] Apache[2.4.52], WordPress[6.4.2], PHP[8.1.2], jQuery[3.7.1], Title[My Blog]
use_case: Identify the technology stack to find version-specific vulnerabilities and appropriate testing tools.

name: NFS Share Enumeration
description: Lists exported NFS shares on a target. Misconfigured NFS can expose sensitive files to anyone.
use_case: Check for NFS shares that might be mountable — often contain sensitive data or SSH keys.
detections: NFS mount requests from unauthorized IPs
remediation: Restrict NFS exports to specific IPs
common_mistakes: Not trying to mount the shares (mount -t nfs <target>:/share /mnt)

name: SMTP User Enumeration
description: Enumerates valid email users on an SMTP server using VRFY, EXPN, or RCPT TO commands.
expected_output:
192.168.1.10: admin exists
192.168.1.10: root exists
192.168.1.10: postmaster exists
use_case: Discover valid email addresses for phishing or password attacks against mail servers.
common_mistakes: Only trying VRFY (try RCPT TO if VRFY disabled)
